query: finished my bachelor elsewhere — what do i actually need to get into a master here, and can someone just do one semester as a guest?

queries: finished my bachelor elsewhere — what do i actually need to get into a master here, and can someone just do one semester as a guest?

retrieved.title: Additional master programme
retrieved.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c807419)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c807424)

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
retrieved.text:
Complete the required supplementary examinations in the non-degree programme. Only supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

- [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)
- [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

/

- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have already completed examinations in the non-degree programme and supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- Which supplementary examinations do I have to make up for?

  You can find the required supplementary examinations in your rejection letter. If you have any questions, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) in which the course if offered.

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
retrieved.text:
- [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

/

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
retrieved.text:
/

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. You can only complete the examinations from an EC as lecture examination, not as module examinations. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna,

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

retrieved.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
retrieved.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text:
/

- Qualitative admission requirements (QZB)

  Admission to a master programme is only possible if you have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme. For some master programmes, you must also fulfil so-called qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen", QZB) when submitting your application. QZB are requirement for admission. You can therefore not make up for these as part of the degree programme you are applying for (e.g. as supplementary examinations).

  QZB are a certain number of ECTS that you must have already completed in parts of your previous degree programme. In addition, individual documents may also be required from you, e.g. a description of qualifications or proof of language proficiency. You can find out whether QZB are required for your master programme either in the [curriculum of the respective degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (in German only) or in the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271).

  Related entries:
  - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31175&cHash=4eba972ee804cb8bd7bcba120d0149d6)
  - [Master programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31177&cHash=4a88be10c3b363efc2e78dc594e8873a)
  - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31903&cHash=b8933888e680ba23ff94674d4d28a5dc)
  - [Supplementary examinations (in the admission letter of master programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39933&cHash=62657c685b836056f2e228ca3e437c2e)

retrieved.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
retrieved.text:
As soon as you have completed all qualitative admission requirements, you have to submit an application for admission to the degree programme within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) of a semester. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

- [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)
- [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.text: programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/) - Master programme #### Bachelor programme of at least four years from Bangladesh: Your application can be processed if you - have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years (full-time) at a recognised university in Section 1 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND - have achieved a CGPA of 3.75 (out of 4.0) during your degree programme. Each application has to be processed individually. If you have a **lower CGPA** OR have completed your degree programme at a **university in Sections 2, 3 and 4**, **you do not meet the requirements** for admission to a master programme at the University of Vienna. Your application will therefore be rejected. This applies regardless of your result in the competence check. --- #### Completion of a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years AND a master programme of two years: Your application can be processed if you: - have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years (full-time) at a recognised university AND - have achieved a CGPA of at least 3.75 (out of 4.0) in your bachelor programme AND - have completed a master programme with a stipulated duration of at least two years (full-time) at a recognised university AND - have achieved a CGPA of at least 3.75 out of 4.0 in your

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
retrieved.text: bachelor programme in Afghanistan, you cannot be admitted to a master programme at the University of Vienna. The differences regarding the level of education are too large. Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your bachelor degree certificate is from Afghanistan and you have also already completed a master programme you can apply for admission to a master programme. Each application has to be processed individually. - Bangladesh Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - India Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - Iran If you did not complete a bachelor programme with a duration of at least three years (or at least 180 ECTS) you can also apply for admission with an Associate Degree combined with a bachelor programme with a duration of at least two years. Each application has to be processed individually. - Pakistan Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/).

retrieved.title: Additional master programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

reranked.text:
- [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

/

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text: degree programmes are favoured in the allocation of course places. It may well be that you do not obtain a spot. | | You can follow the recommend study path, complete a degree programme and graduate. | You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible. | | You may be eligible to receive student discounts and financial assistance. Please inform yourself at the responsible authority in advance. | There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority up front. |

reranked.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
reranked.text:
/

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. You can only complete the examinations from an EC as lecture examination, not as module examinations. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. Related links: - [Master Access Guide (MAG)](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271) - [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876049) - [List of all curricula](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (German only) - Diploma programme In 1999, the Bologna Process was initiated by the European Higher Education Area (EHEA). This fundamentally changed the university structure in Austria. Diploma programmes have been replaced by bachelor and master programmes. At the University of Vienna,

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text: Please take note of the differences as well as the pros and cons of both options. | Degree programme | Non-degree programme | | --- | --- | | You are admitted to a specific degree programme. | You are admitted to "attending individual courses" ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen"). | | You are bound to the [admission procedure](/en/admission-procedure/) of the respective degree programme. | You only need your valid passport or personal ID card for admission. | | If applicable, you have to participate in the [entrance exam/selection procedure](/en/entrance-exam/). | You do not have to participate in the entrance exam/selection procedure. | | The amount of the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) depends on your citizenship and duration of studies. | You pay the tuition fee and Students' Union fee, irrespective of your citizenship. | | With regard to courses, you are bound to the order determined in the curriculum (e.g. completing the [STEOP](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/)). | You are able to choose courses more freely. | | Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. Therefore, you have better chances of obtaining a spot. | Students admitted to degree programmes are favoured in the allocation of course places. It may well be that you do not obtain a spot. | | You can follow the recommend study path, complete a degree programme and graduate. | You are only

reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text: over. Can I be admitted to the degree programme? You can only be admitted to a degree programme, if you have applied for admission to the degree programme within the application period. If you have missed this deadline, you have to wait for the application period of the next semester. - Why is the admission period still open, even though the semester has already started? This is a legal regulation that offers prospective students the opportunity to start their studies even after the semester has started. Reasons for this are, for example, obtaining the secondary-school leaving certificate or bachelor degree certificate late or the duration of the processing of applications. If you apply for and are admitted to your degree programme late, you have to expect that the choice of courses will be limited. Registration for courses with compulsory attendance takes place before/at the beginning of the semester. If you have any questions in this regarding this, please contact the responsible [StudiesServiceCenter/StudiesServiceUnit.](https://ssc.univie.ac.at/)

reranked.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
reranked.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text:
/

- Qualitative admission requirements (QZB)

  Admission to a master programme is only possible if you have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme. For some master programmes, you must also fulfil so-called qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen", QZB) when submitting your application. QZB are requirement for admission. You can therefore not make up for these as part of the degree programme you are applying for (e.g. as supplementary examinations).

  QZB are a certain number of ECTS that you must have already completed in parts of your previous degree programme. In addition, individual documents may also be required from you, e.g. a description of qualifications or proof of language proficiency. You can find out whether QZB are required for your master programme either in the [curriculum of the respective degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (in German only) or in the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271).

  Related entries:
  - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31175&cHash=4eba972ee804cb8bd7bcba120d0149d6)
  - [Master programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31177&cHash=4a88be10c3b363efc2e78dc594e8873a)
  - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31903&cHash=b8933888e680ba23ff94674d4d28a5dc)
  - [Supplementary examinations (in the admission letter of master programmes)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=39933&cHash=62657c685b836056f2e228ca3e437c2e)

reranked.title: Non-degree programme to make up for qualitative admission requirements
reranked.text:
As soon as you have completed all qualitative admission requirements, you have to submit an application for admission to the degree programme within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) of a semester. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

reranked.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
reranked.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
Which degree programme are you interested in?
reranked.text: programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/) - Master programme #### Bachelor programme of at least four years from Bangladesh: Your application can be processed if you - have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years (full-time) at a recognised university in Section 1 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND - have achieved a CGPA of 3.75 (out of 4.0) during your degree programme. Each application has to be processed individually. If you have a **lower CGPA** OR have completed your degree programme at a **university in Sections 2, 3 and 4**, **you do not meet the requirements** for admission to a master programme at the University of Vienna. Your application will therefore be rejected. This applies regardless of your result in the competence check. --- #### Completion of a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years AND a master programme of two years: Your application can be processed if you: - have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least four years (full-time) at a recognised university AND - have achieved a CGPA of at least 3.75 (out of 4.0) in your bachelor programme AND - have completed a master programme with a stipulated duration of at least two years (full-time) at a recognised university AND - have achieved a CGPA of at least 3.75 out of 4.0 in your

Your application for admission will therefore be rejected. --- Exception: If your bachelor degree certificate is from Afghanistan and you have also already completed a master programme you can apply for admission to a master programme. Each application has to be processed individually. - Bangladesh Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - India Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/). - Iran If you did not complete a bachelor programme with a duration of at least three years (or at least 180 ECTS) you can also apply for admission with an Associate Degree combined with a bachelor programme with a duration of at least two years. Each application has to be processed individually. - Pakistan Please read the [information about the required CGPA and competence check](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/vfs-global/).

reranked.title: Additional master programme
reranked.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Your documents have to be legalised by [apostille](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121828) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

/

- Bachelor/diploma programme

  If your secondary-school leaving certificate (12 years of education) is from India and you have achieved at least 85% of the maximum possible marks, your application can be processed. Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to a bachelor or diploma programme](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
- Master programme

  Your application can be processed if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - your university is classified as Section 1 or 2 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 60% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  OR, if:

  - You have completed a bachelor programme with a stipulated duration of at least three years (full-time) at a recognised university AND
  - you have completed your bachelor programme at a state university, which is classified as Section 3 (according to ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA Credential Evaluation) AND
  - you have achieved at least 80% of the maximum points available in your studies.

  Each application must be reviewed individually.

  [Detailed information about admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

heading_path: Master programmes with entrance exam procedure
reranked.text: programme. Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted". 2. Complete the entrance exam procedure of the chosen degree programme. The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

selected.title: Additional master programme
selected.text:
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c807419)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c807424)

selected.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
selected.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

selected.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
selected.text:
Complete the required supplementary examinations in the non-degree programme. Only supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

- [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)
- [Tips on how to create your semester schedule](/en/semester-planning/)
- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)
- [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

/

- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have already completed examinations in the non-degree programme and supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- Which supplementary examinations do I have to make up for?

  You can find the required supplementary examinations in your rejection letter. If you have any questions, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) in which the course if offered.

- [Detailed info on the u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en)
- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)
- [Admission to master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

/

- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have already completed examinations in the non-degree programme and supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.
- I am not able to register for a course. How shall I proceed?

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- Which supplementary examinations do I have to make up for?

  You can find the required supplementary examinations in your rejection letter. If you have any questions, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) in which the course if offered.

[5] ABC of terminology — V
reading rooms for studying. External persons can also borrow books. Related Links: - [Vienna University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/) - [u:card for external library users](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/bibliotheksausweis.html) Related entries: - [Special libraries](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29980&cHash=1c9c65775b66f5cff220ddbf18110d0e) - Visiting Master The Visiting Master allows students of a master programme without entrance exam procedure to complete one semester at the University of Vienna. Applicants have to meet certain admission requirements, e.g. they must present a letter of recommendation from their home university. Related Links: - [Visiting Master](/en/admission/master-programmes/visiting-master/) Related entries: - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06)

- [Studying and Living - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/)

/

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

2.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?

4.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Additional master programme
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c807419)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c807424)

[2] Information for asylum seekers — How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

[3] Change of master programme
The admission to a master programme is only possible after completing a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline. In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/) you can find out which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme.

[### Master programmes with entrance exam procedure](#c806701)

[### Master programmes without entrance exam procedure](#c806706)

[4] Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
Complete the required supplementary examinations in the non-degree programme. Only supplementary examinations to the extent of 30 ECTS or less remain? Apply for admission to the desired [master programme](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/) in u:space within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

Question: finished my bachelor elsewhere — what do i actually need to get into a master here, and can someone just do one semester as a guest?

generate.raw_response:
To get into a master programme at the University of Vienna, you need to have completed a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline [1][2][3]. You can check the Master Access Guide to see which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme [1][3]. 

Yes, someone can do one semester as a guest through the Visiting Master programme, which allows students to complete one semester at the University of Vienna without entrance exam procedure, given they meet certain admission requirements, such as a letter of recommendation from their home university [5].

answer:
To get into a master programme at the University of Vienna, you need to have completed a subject-relevant bachelor or diploma programme in the same or a related discipline [1][2][3]. You can check the Master Access Guide to see which bachelor/diploma degree programmes qualify for admission to a specific master programme [1][3]. 

Yes, someone can do one semester as a guest through the Visiting Master programme, which allows students to complete one semester at the University of Vienna without entrance exam procedure, given they meet certain admission requirements, such as a letter of recommendation from their home university [5].
